input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on macOS, targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (currently Xcode 16.x).
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install        # installs Command Line Tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version` should print the version.

---

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **.NET 9 SDK** (or .NET 8 LTS).
- Use the **macOS installer** (`.pkg` for Arm64 or x64 depending on your Mac).
- **Discover versions:** The download page always shows the latest recommended SDK. For MAUI compatibility, check the [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases).
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version` and `dotnet --list-sdks`

---

## 3. Install .NET MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- **Discover what's available:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` should show `maui` as installed.

---

## 4. Install JDK (for Android)

The MAUI workload installs a Microsoft OpenJDK automatically. If you need to manage it manually:

- **Source:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) — JDK 17 is recommended.
- **Verify:** `java -version` (should show 17.x).

---

## 5. Install Android SDK & Tooling

The MAUI workload **auto-provisions** the Android SDK on first build. To manage manually or verify:

- The Android SDK gets installed to `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx/` (or similar).
- Accept licenses:
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
    -f net9.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
- **Verify:** The `sdkmanager --list` command (inside the SDK's `cmdline-tools/latest/bin/`) shows installed packages.

---

## 6. (Optional but Recommended) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + Extensions marketplace |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued) | No longer supported — use VS Code or JetBrains Rider |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

For VS Code, install these extensions:
- **C# Dev Kit** (includes C# + IntelliSense)
- **.NET MAUI** extension

---

## 7. Set Up iOS Simulators & Android Emulators

**iOS:**
```bash
xcrun simctl list devices    # lists available simulators
# Download more runtimes via Xcode → Settings → Platforms
```

**Android:**
```bash
# Create an emulator (after Android SDK is provisioned)
$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/avdmanager \
  create avd -n "Pixel_API_35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;arm64-v8a"

$ANDROID_HOME/emulator/emulator -avd Pixel_API_35
```

---

## 8. End-to-End Verification

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for all targets
dotnet build

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

If both launch successfully, your environment is fully configured.

---

## Quick Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| Component | How to find the right version |
|-----------|-------------------------------|
| .NET SDK | `dot.net/download` — latest SDK for your MAUI target |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload search maui` — matches your SDK |
| Xcode | Apple requires latest for App Store submissions |
| Android API level | [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) list supported targets |
| JDK | Auto-installed with MAUI; JDK 17 if manual |
